summary: The Czech Republic’s prime minister urged the central bank to cut interest rates, doubling down on his push for lower borrowing costs even as the country faces growing inflation risks from energy prices.

summary: Guinea, the world’s biggest bauxite producer, plans to announce reforms next month to control exports of the ore used in making aluminum as it bids to bolster prices.

summary: Nigeria’s economy grew at a slower-than-expected pace in the first quarter as growth in both its oil and non-oil sectors eased.

title_cn: 伊朗战争挤压印度天然气电力供应，需求创历史新高
summary: India’s gas power generation has plunged to the lowest level in at least six years, with the Iran war hitting fuel shipments, straining electricity supplies at a time when a scorching summer is pushing demand to record highs.

title_cn: 能源、芯片领涨 意大利股市创 26 年来首次新高
summary: Italy’s benchmark equity index rose past its all-time closing high set in 2000, with a recent rally in energy and chip stocks supercharging it to record levels.

title_cn: 印度五月第四次提高柴油和汽油价格
summary: India’s state-run fuel retailers raised gasoline and diesel prices for the fourth time in 10 days in response to disruptions in global energy markets, a move that risks exacerbating inflationary risks in the economy.

title_cn: Zepto 计划 6 月公开申请 10 亿美元印度 IPO
summary: Rapid-commerce firm Zepto Ltd. is preparing to publicly file in the first half of June for an initial public offering that may raise up to $1 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Iran Says US Deal Not Imminent | The Pulse 5/25/2026
title_cn: 伊朗表示美国不会立即达成协议脉搏 2026 年 5 月 25 日
summary:
Iran has denied that a deal to end the war with the US is imminent, after US officials said they were closing in on a deal. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said an agreement could come as soon as Monday, but Iran’s Foreign Ministry Spokesman Esmail Baghaei said “It is true that a consensus was reached on many of the topics discussed, but no one can claim that the signing of an agreement is imminent.”

Turkish police entered the headquarters of the main opposition CHP party following a court decision last week, that removed Ozgur Ozel as its leader. The move deepens a political crisis that saw a market selloff last week.

And Huawei says it's made a breakthrough in making advanced semiconductors without cutting-edge equipment, potentially reducing the gap with industry leader TSMC.

Today's guests: Marco Valli, UniCredit, Chief European Economist & Alex Karnal, Braidwell, Co-Founder and CIO. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Italy Tightens Grip on Payments Company Nexi With Stake Increase
title_cn: 意大利通过增持支付公司 Nexi 加强控制
summary: Italy’s state lender Cassa Depositi e Prestiti SpA is set to increase its stake in Nexi SpA, tightening its grip on the payment company considered strategic by Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni’s government.

title_cn: 软银发行 16 亿美元日元债券再次进军零售市场
summary: SoftBank Group Corp. plans to raise ¥260 billion ($1.6 billion) through another sale of subordinated bonds aimed mainly at individual investors, about two months after a similar retail offering.

title: Iran Says US Deal Not Imminent | The Opening Trade 5/25/2026
title_cn: 伊朗表示美国不会立即达成协议开盘交易 2026 年 5 月 25 日
summary:
Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman says a deal to open the Strait of Hormuz is not imminent, but that consensus has been reached on many issues. Earlier, oil fell after senior US officials said Washington and Tehran were closing in on an agreement to open the vital waterway.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Joumanna Bercetche and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India Bond Investors Tap Soaring Swap Rates to Juice Returns
title_cn: 印度债券投资者利用飙升的掉期利率来增加回报
summary: Multi-year high swap rates are driving some Indian debt fund managers into trades aimed at boosting fixed-income returns.

title: Swiss Trader Had Lucrative Role Getting Iraqi Oil Through Hormuz

summary: A little-known Swiss trading company played a key role in the transit through the Strait of Hormuz of an oil supertanker whose stop-start journey captivated the oil market earlier this month, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: BlackRock’s Saigal Sees ‘Sufficient Factors’ to Justify Fed Cut
title_cn: 贝莱德的 Saigal 认为有“足够的因素”来证明美联储降息的合理性
summary: The Federal Reserve may have enough reason to justify an interest rate cut rather than a hike under new chairman Kevin Warsh, according to BlackRock Inc.

title: What It Takes to Run One of London's Most Popular Pubs | Odd Lots
title_cn: 经营伦敦最受欢迎的酒吧之一需要什么？零股
summary: Restaurants are great microcosms for macro-economic trends. They sit at the intersection of everything from consumer confidence to commodity costs to the labor market. According to the British Beer and Pub Association, approximately two pubs a day have closed in England during the first quarter of 2026. Could pubs tell us something about larger trends in the British economy? We talk to Oisin Rogers and Ashley Palmer-Watts, co-founders of The Devonshire, a famed London pub, top find out. Rogers, the publican, discusses the difference between a good and bad pub, why he hates the word 'gastropub,' and how the indoor smoking ban changed the meaning of pubs for the average Londoner. In a segment from our London live show, chef Palmer-Watts, details the factors that lead to a perfect pint of Guinness, why higher ingredient costs does not always correlate to higher menu prices, and about making a Victorian-era meat fruit for Apple's Jony Ive. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Gains as Prospects of Iran Deal Temper Inflation Concerns
title_cn: 伊朗协议的前景缓解了通胀担忧，金价上涨
summary: Gold advanced as signs the US and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz tempered inflation concerns.

title: China Cracks Down on Illegal Cross-Border Trading
title_cn: 中国严厉打击非法跨境贸易
summary: China has launched an unprecedented campaign against illegal cross-border trading to stem capital outflows, threatening severe penalties against popular brokers and ordering non-compliant accounts to be liquidated within two years. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China’s Solar Installations Fall for Fourth Straight Month
title_cn: 中国太阳能装机量连续第四个月下降
summary: China’s new solar installations fell for a fourth straight month in April, underscoring persistent weakness in domestic demand.

title: South Africa’s Pick n Pay Slumps After New Target Delayed

summary: South Africa’s Pick n Pay Stores Ltd. fell after it delayed a key turnaround target by a year, tempering optimism that had driven the grocer’s stock higher ahead of the results.

title: Prosus Asks EU to End Forced Sale of Delivery Hero Stake
title_cn: 普罗苏斯要求欧盟终止强制出售“送货英雄”股份
summary: Prosus NV has asked the European Union to drop a requirement that it sell shares in Delivery Hero SE, part of antitrust approval for its acquisition of another delivery platform, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Pope Says AI Should Be Disarmed to Avoid Dominating Humanity
title_cn: 教皇称人工智能应该被解除武装以避免统治人类
summary: Pope Leo XIV said artificial intelligence should be “disarmed” to protect humanity from its dangers, adding his voice to a heated debate over the extent to which governments should regulate a technology that is reshaping the world.

From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

(Source: Bloomberg)

summary: SpaceX is marketing itself to IPO investors as an artificial intelligence targeting a $26.5 trillion potential market opportunity. Bloomberg Market Structure Research Head Larry Tabb joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: Mexico’s trade surplus widened far more than expected in April to $4.52 billion, well above the median estimate of $476.35 million in a Bloomberg survey, as exports climbed to the highest since the data began in 1980. The surge highlights how the country continues to strengthen its role as a manufacturing hub for the US economy as Washington takes a tougher stance on trade. title: Trump Says Iran Talks ‘Proceeding Nicely’ as Deal Appears Closer
title_cn: 特朗普称伊朗谈判“进展顺利”，协议似乎更接近
summary: US President Donald Trump said negotiations with Iran over an interim deal to extend their ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz were “proceeding nicely.”

title: Trump Pushes to Quiet GOP Critics of Iranian Dealmaking
title_cn: 特朗普敦促共和党对伊朗交易的批评保持沉默
summary: The president urges supporters to not “listen to the losers”

title: Kenya Treasury Warns Budget Strain May Force Spending Cuts
title_cn: 肯尼亚财政部警告预算紧张可能迫使削减支出
summary: Kenya may be forced to cut spending in its next fiscal budget as rising debt-service costs, limited tax options and tighter borrowing constraints strain the Treasury’s financing plans.

title: Xi Thanks Pakistan’s Sharif for Mediating in Iran Conflict
title_cn: 习近平感谢巴基斯坦谢里夫调解伊朗冲突
summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping thanked visiting Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif for Pakistan’s work in mediating talks between the US and Iran — underscoring Beijing’s push to end a war that poses a range of risks to the world’s second-biggest economy.

title: Japan Won’t Add Bonds on Calendar Basis to Fund Extra Budget
title_cn: 日本不会按日历增加债券来为额外预算提供资金
summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said the government will finance its extra budget without increasing bond issuance on a calendar basis, in a likely attempt to ease market concerns over government finances.

title: Oil Sands Firms Can Afford Carbon Capture, Canadian Energy Minister Says
title_cn: 加拿大能源部长表示，油砂公司有能力承担碳捕获费用
summary: Canadian Energy Minister Tim Hodgson said he’s “highly confident” that Alberta oil sands companies can absorb the cost of building carbon capture, despite their protests that Canada’s climate rules hurt their global competitiveness.

title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Faces Toughest Test Yet
title_cn: 伊朗最高领袖面临迄今为止最严峻的考验
summary: Tehran wants to show that Mojtaba Khamenei is the one with the final say over major state matters.

title: Implosion of Senegal Administration Muddies Debt Talks With IMF
title_cn: 塞内加尔政府的内爆扰乱了与国际货币基金组织的债务谈判
summary: Senegal President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s abrupt dismissal of Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko risks throwing the government’s stalled talks with the International Monetary Fund into deeper disarray.

title: Wins for Ireland’s Fine Gael and Social Democrats
title_cn: 爱尔兰统一党和社会民主党获胜
summary: Fine Gael, a member of Ireland’s governing coalition, and the Social Democrats won seats in two by-elections Sunday.

title: Pope Urges Leaders Not to Use Wars to Distract From Other Issues
title_cn: 教宗敦促领导人不要利用战争来分散对其他问题的注意力
summary: Pope Leo XIV warned world leaders not to use wars as a political tactic to distract voters from their domestic problems.

title: Starmer Leadership Strife Reignites UK Wealth Tax Debate
title_cn: 斯塔默领导层之争重新引发英国财富税争论
summary: The leadership crisis around Prime Minister Keir Starmer has renewed a debate over how the British government should raise revenue, and given those on the left of the ruling Labour party confidence to promote their pet cause: wealth taxes.

title: Ireland Must Address Economic Vulnerabilities, IMF Warns
title_cn: 国际货币基金组织警告爱尔兰必须解决经济脆弱性
summary: Ireland cannot take its fiscal resilience for granted, the International Monetary Fund warned, as unpredictable geopolitical events elevate uncertainty even in one of Europe’s strongest economies.

title: India’s Finance Minister Defends Modi’s Plan to Push Austerity
title_cn: 印度财政部长捍卫莫迪推动紧缩计划
summary: India’s finance minister defended Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s call for austerity during the Iran war after opposition parties criticized the appeal, saying the conflict was creating external pressures on the economy.

title: Iran War: Trump Says 'No Rush' as US, Iran Inch Towards Deal | Daybreak Europe 05/25/2026
title_cn: 伊朗战争：随着美国和伊朗逐步达成协议，特朗普表示“不着急”欧洲黎明 05/25/2026
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

The US and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, senior US officials said Sunday, even as President Donald Trump insisted he would not “rush” into an agreement.

Optimism over a deal pushed global stocks towards a record high as crude oil fell. Secretary of State Marco Rubio struck a cautiously upbeat tone, saying the US was going to give diplomacy every chance. “We thought we might have some news last night,” he told reporters in New Delhi. “Maybe today.”

Today's guests: Charu Chanana, Saxo, Chief Investment Strategist; Máximo Torero, Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, Chief Economist.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Polymarket Banned in Indonesia After Bets on Prabowo’s Ouster
title_cn: 押注普拉博沃下台后印尼Polymarket被禁止
summary: Indonesia blocked access to Polymarket, labeling the prediction-market a form of online gambling soon after a bet on the possible early end of President Prabowo Subianto’s tenure circulated widely on social media.

title: US, Iran Inch Toward Deal as Gaps Remain on Uranium, Sanctions
title_cn: 由于铀和制裁方面仍存在分歧，美国和伊朗正在逐步达成协议
summary: The US and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, senior US officials said Sunday, even as President Donald Trump insisted he would not “rush” into an agreement.

title: Xi Attacked Japan’s Rearmament During Trump Summit, FT Says
title_cn: 英国《金融时报》称习近平在特朗普峰会期间攻击日本重新军备
summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping criticized Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi for her defense push during his summit with US President Donald Trump in Beijing, the Financial Times reported, citing people familiar with the meeting.

title: Indonesia to Lay Out Commodity Export Policy Soon, Official Says
title_cn: 官员表示，印度尼西亚将很快制定大宗商品出口政策
summary: Global markets and major trading partners can expect updates on Indonesia’s new commodity export policy within weeks as the government works on the legal and structural rollout of its new centralized export agency, according to a trade ministry official.

title: Benin’s Wadagni Names Former IMF Economist as Finance Minister
title_cn: 贝宁瓦达尼任命前国际货币基金组织经济学家为财政部长
summary: Benin’s new president, Romuald Wadagni, has appointed a finance ministry veteran and former economist at the International Monetary Fund as the country’s new finance chief.

title: New Zealand to Ease Golden Visa Investment Criteria to Allow Philanthropy
title_cn: 新西兰放宽黄金签证投资标准以允许慈善事业
summary: New Zealand will let wealthy migrants count philanthropic donations toward the investment requirement of its most popular golden visa category, widening a program that has attracted billions of dollars in pledged investment.

title: Hassett Says Ending Iran War May Create Room for Fed Rate Cut
title_cn: 哈塞特称结束伊朗战争可能为美联储降息创造空间
summary: Kevin Hassett, President Donald Trump’s chief economic adviser at the White House, signaled he’s confident that an eventual drop in oil prices will create space for the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates.

title: Senegal Parliament Speaker, Ally of Former PM Sonko, Resigns
title_cn: 塞内加尔议会议长、前总理桑科的盟友辞职
summary: Senegal’s parliament speaker Malick Ndiaye resigned on Sunday, becoming the latest senior figure caught in the widening rupture at the top of the country’s ruling movement.

title: Bolivia’s Paz Says Protests Test Democracy Ahead of Talks
title_cn: 玻利维亚总统帕兹称抗议活动在会谈前考验民主
summary: Anti-government protests and persistent roadblocks in Bolivia are testing the country’s transition to a democracy that’s open to the world economy, embattled President Rodrigo Paz said in an interview with Bloomberg Television’s Wall Street Week.

title: Trump Touts Influence in Midterm Primaries
title_cn: 特朗普在中期初选中宣扬影响力
summary: Trump jumped in to the Texas runoff as he and his political team are feeling emboldened by having recently vanquished Republican candidates in Indiana and Louisiana deemed insufficiently loyal. Bloomberg Texas Bureau Chief Julie Fine joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss Trump endorsing Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in the upcoming GOP Senate primary runoff over Senator John Cornyn. (Source: Bloomberg)
